description: Propose recommendations for the seeded voucher-dropoff anomaly. Inputs are the Anomaly + reference Diagnosis (Path C). PR F established this fixture passes 5/5 (100%) on the rubric.

anomaly.impact: payment_value for payment_type:voucher fell ~95% in a sustained dropoff. Investigate the root cause.

golden_output.title: Relaunch voucher program with a targeted re-engagement broadcast to lapsed voucher users
golden_output.rationale: The diagnosis confirms the voucher payment collapse is program-level (suspended, expired, or rule-changed) — not demand-driven. The 12,500 customers who were active voucher payers in weeks 1–9 did NOT fully migrate to other payment types; ~8% of their order volume is simply lost. A broadcast campaign announcing a relaunched voucher program (or a bridge alternative-payment incentive) is the fastest lever to recover this audience before churn becomes permanent.
steps: 1. Define the target audience: customers who completed ≥1 order with payment_type=voucher between 2025-12-01 and 2026-02-01 (weeks 1–9 baseline window) AND have placed 0 voucher orders since 2026-02-02.
2. Confirm internally whether the voucher program has been restored, updated, or replaced — this determines the campaign message (relaunch vs. alternative incentive).

3. Draft a single email/push broadcast: subject line 'Your voucher payment option is back — here's what changed' (or 'A new way to save' if pivoting to a new program); include clear CTA to browse and pay.
4. Schedule the broadcast for a Tuesday or Wednesday AM send to the ~12,500 identified customers.
5. Set UTM parameters (utm_campaign=voucher_relaunch, utm_medium=email) to track attributed orders in your analytics dashboard.
6. Monitor voucher order_count daily for 7 days post-send; compare to the weeks-10–26 baseline of ~3 orders/week.

estimatedImpact.range: +R$50,700 – R$101,400 recovered (~$10k–$20k USD) in first 2 weeks post-send
estimatedImpact.assumption: Assumes 10–20% reactivation rate of 12,500 affected customers placing 1 order at R$434 AOV (platform average). Lower bound = 1,250 reactivated × R$434; upper = 2,500 × R$434. USD at 5:1 BRL.

prerequisites.label: Email or push channel active for customer base
Voucher program restored or replacement incentive defined
Customer event history queryable to build weeks-1–9 voucher-payer segment

golden_output.successMetric: Voucher order_count recovers from the post-cliff baseline of ~3 orders/week to ≥20 orders/week within 14 days of campaign send, measured via weekly order_count filtered by payment_type=voucher.

golden_output.title: Create a 'Lapsed Voucher Payer' win-back scenario with a time-limited alternative-payment voucher
golden_output.rationale: A one-shot broadcast captures immediate re-engagement, but the 17-week sustained gap means many customers have already formed new habits or disengaged. An automated win-back scenario with a time-limited discount voucher (usable with credit_card or boleto) bridges the gap while the voucher program is being fixed — and converts at-risk churners before they're permanently lost. This is the highest-leverage automation to run in parallel with the broadcast.
steps: 1. Create a new triggered scenario: entry condition = customer has payment_type=voucher in any order before 2026-02-02 AND zero orders of any payment type in the last 60 days.

2. Build a 3-touch drip: Day 0 — email 'We miss you + here's 10% off your next order (any payment method)'; Day 7 — push/SMS reminder if no purchase; Day 14 — final email with urgency ('Offer expires in 48 hours').
3. Attach a voucher pool: single-use 10% discount codes, capped at R$80 per redemption, valid for 21 days from issue date.
4. Set scenario exit condition: customer places any order OR voucher expires.
5. Cap frequency to 1 scenario enrollment per customer to respect communication limits.
6. Activate the scenario and let it run for 60 days, then evaluate conversion and margin impact.

estimatedImpact.assumption: Assumes 6–12% conversion of 12,500 lapsed customers (750–1,500 orders) at R$434 AOV, net of 10% voucher discount (effective AOV ~R$390). USD at 5:1 BRL. Overlapping audience with campaign rec above; additive only for customers not reached by broadcast.

prerequisites.label: Voucher pool (10% off, single-use codes) — must be created

golden_output.successMetric: Win-back scenario achieves ≥8% conversion (orders placed) within 30 days of enrollment, measured as scenario_converted_customers ÷ scenario_entered_customers. Baseline: 0% (no current win-back running for this segment).

golden_output.title: A/B test alternative payment-method incentives to identify the best credit_card / boleto migration path
golden_output.rationale: If the voucher program cannot be fully restored, the merchant needs to migrate the 12,500 affected customers to credit_card or boleto without permanent revenue loss. The diagnosis shows substitution did NOT happen organically — meaning customers need an active push. An experiment will reveal whether a payment-method incentive (e.g., 'Pay with credit card, get free shipping') outperforms a blanket discount, de-risking the decision before scaling spend.
steps: 1. Split the 12,500 lapsed voucher-payer segment randomly into 3 cohorts (~4,167 each): Arm A = 10% discount on next order (any payment method); Arm B = free shipping when paying by credit card; Arm C = control (no incentive, just a re-engagement email).
2. Send the experiment via email broadcast on the same day for all arms; keep subject line identical except for the offer copy.
3. Track for 14 days: primary metric = order_count per arm; secondary = payment_type distribution (did credit_card/boleto share rise?) and revenue per arm.
4. Declare a winner if any arm achieves ≥95% statistical significance on order_count lift vs. control.
5. Roll out the winning incentive to the remaining audience and encode it into the win-back scenario (Recommendation 2) for ongoing use.
6. Document the experiment result in the voucher-program post-mortem to inform future program design.

estimatedImpact.range: +R$18,228 – R$54,684 incremental (~$3.6k–$10.9k USD) over 14-day test window
estimatedImpact.assumption: Winning arm expected to convert 10–20% of its ~4,167-customer cohort (417–833 orders) at R$434 AOV. Experiment cost = discount/shipping subsidy on redeemed orders. USD at 5:1 BRL. Full-rollout upside is captured in Rec 1 & 2 estimates above.

prerequisites.label: A/B experiment / split-send capability in email platform
Voucher pool or shipping-subsidy mechanism for Arm A/B incentives
Lapsed voucher-payer segment built (shared prerequisite with Rec 1)

golden_output.successMetric: Winning arm achieves statistically significant (p<0.05) lift in 14-day order_count vs. control arm; target ≥15% conversion in the winning arm vs. ≤5% in control. Baseline: ~3 voucher orders/week across the full base (~0.024% weekly order rate).

scoring_config.notes: Same required fields. Expected recommendation thrust: relaunch / win-back / A-B-test voucher mechanisms (per PR F's spot-check sample 2).
